drafts.x:
10Y yield is surging — and the other signals aren't confirming.
Needs oil to break the range — only then does it matter.

SPX 7,408. 10Y 4.59%. Oil $100.

• 10Y moves beyond 4.79% or below 4.39%

What breaks this range — and which direction?
drafts.substack:
REGIME: Transitional | Stable
CONFIDENCE: High (85/100)
RISK TILT: neutral
STABILITY: Stable

Snapshot — May 18, 2026
  S&P 500:     7,408.39   z +1.65
  10Y Yield:      4.585%  z +2.17
  WTI Oil:        99.76   z +0.62

INSIGHT
S&P 500 at 7,408 is pushing above its recent range — a move that only matters if it holds. 10Y yield at 4.59% is running well beyond its 63-session range — yields haven't been this stretched in months. Oil at $100 is sitting near its 63-session mean — no signal from oil.

INVALIDATION
• SPX breaks above 7,520 or below 7,297
• 10Y moves beyond 4.79% or below 4.39%

Method: MacroEvidence pipeline · yfinance + FRED · 63-session rolling z-scores · not financial advice.
